name: Jie Deng
title: Power Electronics PhD Researcher
tagline: Exploring medium-voltage converters, transformer co-design, and next-generation power modules for future energy interfaces.
location: Arizona State University, Phoenix, Arizona, United States
researchAreas: Single-port and dual-port SST transformer design
AI-based converter and power module design

hero.headline: I research medium-voltage power converters, transformer co-design, and power modules that can push future energy systems forward.
hero.subheadline: PhD researcher at Arizona State University working at the intersection of solid-state transformers, high-frequency power conversion, insulation-aware design, and next-generation GaN/SiC power modules.

quickFacts.label: Now
quickFacts.detail: Focused on medium-voltage solid-state transformer systems and high-voltage module design.

quickFacts.label: Trajectory
quickFacts.detail: From grid-tied inverters and UPS hardware to high-frequency, high-voltage research platforms.

quickFacts.label: Focus
quickFacts.detail: Studying complete power-electronic systems instead of isolated building blocks.

currentFocus.theme: Medium-voltage solid-state transformer converters, transformer co-design, and high-voltage power-module research for next-generation energy conversion systems.
currentFocus.importance: Future energy infrastructure needs converters that are not only efficient, but also compact, intelligent, insulation-aware, and scalable. By working across SST converters, transformer design, auxiliary supplies, high-voltage modules, and partial-discharge platforms, I aim to help medium-voltage power electronics move closer to deployable systems for cleaner and more flexible grids.
currentFocus.now: My current work spans converter topology design, transformer and insulation constraints, auxiliary power architecture, GaN and SiC module development, partial-discharge experimentation, and AI-assisted design exploration.

projects.title: Medium-voltage SST converter and transformer co-design
projects.summary: Leading an MV SST co-design effort focused on AI-based converter parameter recommendation, high-isolation transformer and auxiliary power hardware, and the insulation bottleneck that has not yet exceeded 20 kV rms AC.

projects.title: High-isolation auxiliary power supply design
projects.summary: Leading a high-isolation auxiliary power project for MV SST systems, with the goal of combining strong insulation capability and high power density; the experimental phase is complete and the work is now moving into paper writing.

projects.title: High-switching-frequency medium-voltage GaN power module design
projects.summary: Leading a 1300 V / 60 A series-stacked GaN power module effort aimed at breaking the 1 MHz switching barrier in medium-voltage power semiconductors; experiments are complete, one conference paper is published, and the next step is moving from discrete devices to bare-die GaN.

projects.title: Medium-frequency high-voltage SiC power module design
projects.summary: Developing high-voltage SiC module concepts for medium-frequency operation with attention to packaging, electric field stress, and system integration.

projects.title: Square-wave partial discharge experimental platform
projects.summary: Leading the development of a square-wave partial-discharge experimental platform to address the lack of commercial equipment for partial-discharge testing under square-wave excitation.

projects.title: AI-based converter and power module design
projects.summary: Investigating how AI methods can accelerate design-space exploration for converters, modules, and system-level decisions.

goals: Bridge converter topology, transformer design, insulation behavior, and module development in one coherent research direction
Build research platforms that produce trustworthy experimental insight at medium voltage
Create designs that are not only high-performance on paper, but meaningful for future deployable power systems

skillGroups.title: Hardware
skillGroups.description: System-level hardware capability in high-power and high-frequency power-electronics converters.

skillGroups.title: Software / EDA and Simulation
skillGroups.description: Schematic, PCB, and circuit-simulation tools used in converter research and prototyping.

skillGroups.title: Software / Coding and Analysis
skillGroups.description: Embedded, digital, AI, and CAE tools used to implement controllers and analyze hardware systems.

cv.profile: I am a PhD researcher in power electronics at Arizona State University. My work connects medium-voltage solid-state transformer systems, transformer and insulation-aware design, high-isolation auxiliary power supplies, and next-generation GaN/SiC power modules.

education.focus: Medium-voltage SST converters and transformer design, high-isolation auxiliary power supplies, GaN/SiC power modules, partial-discharge platforms, and AI-based design

education.institution: Hebei University of Technology, Tianjin, China
education.focus: Three-phase high-frequency T-type three-level grid-connected inverter research above 200 kHz, research writing, soft-switching, and three-level converter technology

education.institution: Changsha University of Science and Technology, Changsha, China
education.focus: Single-phase grid-tied inverters, UPS power supplies, DC-DC converters, auxiliary power supplies, DSP/STM32 programming, and PCB design

strengths: Working across converter, transformer, insulation, and module perspectives rather than treating them as isolated topics
Combining hardware implementation with experimental validation and research writing
Building technical depth from practical converter prototypes toward medium-voltage system research
collaboration: Open to academic collaboration in SSTs, medium-voltage converters, transformer and insulation design, and power modules
Interested in discussions around high-frequency and high-voltage experimental platforms
Available for technical exchange through email or GitHub

contact.title: Interested in collaboration or technical exchange?
contact.text: I am happy to connect around medium-voltage converters, solid-state transformers, power-module design, insulation-aware systems, and AI-based design in power electronics.
